concepts.label: Up Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge +2/3 and mass ~2.2 MeV

concepts.label: Down Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge -1/3 and mass ~4.7 MeV

concepts.label: Charm Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge +2/3 and mass ~1.27 GeV

concepts.label: Strange Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge -1/3 and mass ~96 MeV

concepts.label: Top Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge +2/3 and mass ~173 GeV

concepts.label: Bottom Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental quark with charge -1/3 and mass ~4.18 GeV

concepts.label: Quark
concepts.definition: Fundamental particle that makes up hadrons, comes in 6 flavors

concepts.label: Hadron
concepts.definition: Composite particle made of quarks bound by the strong force

concepts.label: Baryon
concepts.definition: Hadron made of 3 quarks (e.g. proton, neutron)

concepts.label: Meson
concepts.definition: Hadron made of a quark-antiquark pair

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: Baryon with 2 up quarks and 1 down quark, charge +1

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: Baryon with 1 up quark and 2 down quarks, charge 0

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: Stable lepton, orbits atomic nucleus, charge -1

concepts.label: Muon
concepts.definition: Unstable lepton ~207x electron mass, charge -1

concepts.label: Tau
concepts.definition: Heaviest lepton, very short-lived, charge -1

concepts.label: Electron Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with electron, charge 0

concepts.label: Muon Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with muon, charge 0

concepts.label: Tau Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with tau, charge 0

concepts.label: Lepton
concepts.definition: Fundamental particle not subject to the strong force

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: Massless boson mediating electromagnetic force

concepts.label: Gluon
concepts.definition: Boson mediating the strong nuclear force between quarks

concepts.label: W Boson
concepts.definition: Massive boson mediating charged weak interactions

concepts.label: Z Boson
concepts.definition: Massive neutral boson mediating neutral weak interactions

concepts.label: Higgs Boson
concepts.definition: Boson giving mass to particles via Higgs mechanism

concepts.label: Boson
concepts.definition: Force-carrying particle with integer spin

concepts.label: Graviton
concepts.definition: Hypothetical boson mediating gravity, not yet observed

concepts.label: Standard Model
concepts.definition: Theory describing fundamental particles and 3 of 4 fundamental forces

concepts.label: Strong Force
concepts.definition: Strongest fundamental force, binds quarks

concepts.label: Weak Force
concepts.definition: Force responsible for radioactive decay

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Force
concepts.definition: Force between charged particles

concepts.label: Gravity
concepts.definition: Weakest fundamental force, attractive between masses

concepts.label: Fundamental Forces
concepts.definition: The four fundamental interactions of nature

concepts.label: Quantum Mechanics
concepts.definition: Physics of particles at atomic and subatomic scales

concepts.label: Quantum Superposition
concepts.definition: Particle exists in multiple states simultaneously until measured

concepts.label: Quantum Entanglement
concepts.definition: Correlated quantum states where measuring one instantly affects the other

concepts.label: Wave-Particle Duality
concepts.definition: Quantum entities exhibit both wave and particle properties

concepts.label: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
concepts.definition: Cannot simultaneously know exact position and momentum of a particle

concepts.label: Schrödinger Equation
concepts.definition: Fundamental equation describing quantum state evolution over time

concepts.label: Wave Function
concepts.definition: Mathematical description of quantum state of a system

concepts.label: Wave Function Collapse
concepts.definition: Transition from superposition to single eigenstate upon measurement

concepts.label: Quantum Tunneling
concepts.definition: Particle passing through a potential barrier it classically cannot surmount

concepts.label: Quantum Decoherence
concepts.definition: Loss of quantum coherence due to environment interaction

concepts.label: Quantum Field Theory
concepts.definition: Framework combining quantum mechanics and special relativity

concepts.label: Pauli Exclusion Principle
concepts.definition: No two identical fermions can occupy the same quantum state

concepts.label: Spin
concepts.definition: Intrinsic angular momentum of quantum particles

concepts.label: Quantum Number
concepts.definition: Value describing a conserved quantity in a quantum system

concepts.label: Principal Quantum Number
concepts.definition: Quantum number determining electron shell/energy level (n)

concepts.label: Orbital Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Quantum number l describing shape of electron orbital

concepts.label: Magnetic Quantum Number
concepts.definition: Quantum number ml describing orbital orientation in magnetic field

concepts.label: Planck Constant
concepts.definition: Fundamental constant h = 6.626×10⁻³⁴ J·s relating energy to frequency

concepts.label: Photoelectric Effect
concepts.definition: Emission of electrons when light hits a material, explained by photon theory

concepts.label: De Broglie Wavelength
concepts.definition: Wavelength associated with a particle: λ = h/p

concepts.label: Compton Scattering
concepts.definition: Photon scattering off electron with wavelength increase

concepts.label: Double-Slit Experiment
concepts.definition: Experiment showing wave-particle duality via interference pattern

concepts.label: Copenhagen Interpretation
concepts.definition: QM interpretation: wave function collapses upon observation

concepts.label: Many-Worlds Interpretation
concepts.definition: QM interpretation: all possible outcomes occur in branching universes

concepts.label: Quantum Chromodynamics
concepts.definition: Theory of the strong interaction between quarks and gluons

concepts.label: Quantum Electrodynamics
concepts.definition: Quantum theory of electromagnetic interactions

concepts.label: Fermion
concepts.definition: Particle with half-integer spin, obeys Pauli exclusion

concepts.label: Antimatter
concepts.definition: Matter composed of antiparticles with opposite charge

concepts.label: Positron
concepts.definition: Antiparticle of electron with charge +1

concepts.label: Pair Production
concepts.definition: Creation of particle-antiparticle pair from energy

concepts.label: Annihilation
concepts.definition: Particle meets antiparticle, converting to energy

concepts.label: Zero-Point Energy
concepts.definition: Lowest possible energy of a quantum system, not zero

concepts.label: Quantum Computing
concepts.definition: Computing using quantum superposition and entanglement

concepts.label: Qubit
concepts.definition: Quantum bit that can be in superposition of 0 and 1

concepts.label: Bell's Theorem
concepts.definition: No local hidden variable theory can reproduce all quantum predictions

concepts.label: Quantum Teleportation
concepts.definition: Transfer of quantum state using entanglement and classical communication

concepts.label: Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Branch of physics dealing with heat, work, and energy

concepts.label: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: If A is in thermal equilibrium with B and C, then B and C are in equilibrium

concepts.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed (ΔU = Q - W)

concepts.label: Second Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy of an isolated system never decreases

concepts.label: Third Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy approaches zero as temperature approaches absolute zero

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: Measure of disorder or number of accessible microstates in a system

concepts.label: Enthalpy
concepts.definition: Total heat content of a system at constant pressure (H = U + PV)

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: Energy available for work at constant T and P (G = H - TS)

concepts.label: Heat Capacity
concepts.definition: Amount of heat needed to raise temperature by 1 degree

concepts.label: Specific Heat
concepts.definition: Heat capacity per unit mass of a substance

concepts.label: Absolute Zero
concepts.definition: Lowest possible temperature: 0 K = -273.15°C

concepts.label: Carnot Cycle
concepts.definition: Most efficient theoretical thermodynamic cycle between two temperatures

concepts.label: Boltzmann Constant
concepts.definition: Relates average kinetic energy to temperature: k = 1.381×10⁻²³ J/K

concepts.label: Maxwell-Boltzmann Distribution
concepts.definition: Statistical distribution of particle speeds in an ideal gas

concepts.label: Thermal Equilibrium
concepts.definition: State where no net heat flows between systems

concepts.label: Heat Transfer
concepts.definition: Movement of thermal energy from hot to cold body

concepts.label: Conduction
concepts.definition: Heat transfer through direct contact between molecules

concepts.label: Convection
concepts.definition: Heat transfer through bulk fluid movement

concepts.label: Radiation
concepts.definition: Heat transfer via electromagnetic waves

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: PV = nRT, relating pressure, volume, temperature of ideal gas

concepts.label: Adiabatic Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process with no heat exchange

concepts.label: Isothermal Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant temperature

concepts.label: Isobaric Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant pressure

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.definition: Change of matter between solid, liquid, gas states

concepts.label: Latent Heat
concepts.definition: Energy absorbed or released during phase transition without temperature change

concepts.label: Boiling Point
concepts.definition: Temperature at which liquid becomes gas at given pressure

concepts.label: Melting Point
concepts.definition: Temperature at which solid becomes liquid

concepts.label: Triple Point
concepts.definition: Temperature and pressure where solid, liquid, and gas coexist

concepts.label: Critical Point
concepts.definition: Temperature and pressure above which distinct liquid/gas phases don't exist

concepts.label: Electromagnetism
concepts.definition: Fundamental force between electrically charged particles

concepts.label: Electric Field
concepts.definition: Region of space where electric charges experience a force

concepts.label: Magnetic Field
concepts.definition: Region of space where moving charges and magnets experience a force

concepts.label: Maxwell's Equations
concepts.definition: Four equations unifying electricity and magnetism

concepts.label: Coulomb's Law
concepts.definition: Force between charges is proportional to product of charges divided by distance squared

concepts.label: Gauss's Law
concepts.definition: Electric flux through closed surface equals enclosed charge divided by ε₀

concepts.label: Faraday's Law
concepts.definition: Changing magnetic flux induces electromotive force

concepts.label: Ampère's Law
concepts.definition: Magnetic field around a current is proportional to the current

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.definition: Generation of EMF by changing magnetic flux

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Wave
concepts.definition: Self-propagating wave of oscillating electric and magnetic fields

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum
concepts.definition: Range of all electromagnetic radiation frequencies

concepts.label: Radio Waves
concepts.definition: EM waves with longest wavelength, used in communication

concepts.label: Microwaves
concepts.definition: EM waves used in cooking and communication

concepts.label: Infrared
concepts.definition: EM radiation between visible light and microwaves, felt as heat

concepts.label: Visible Light
concepts.definition: EM radiation visible to human eye, 380-700nm

concepts.label: Ultraviolet
concepts.definition: EM radiation beyond violet, causes sunburn

concepts.label: X-Ray
concepts.definition: High-energy EM radiation used in medical imaging

concepts.label: Gamma Ray
concepts.definition: Highest energy EM radiation, from nuclear processes

concepts.label: Electric Current
concepts.definition: Flow of electric charge through a conductor

concepts.label: Voltage
concepts.definition: Electric potential difference between two points

concepts.label: Resistance
concepts.definition: Opposition to electric current flow in a material

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
concepts.definition: V = IR, voltage equals current times resistance

concepts.label: Capacitance
concepts.definition: Ability to store electric charge

concepts.label: Inductance
concepts.definition: Property opposing changes in electric current via magnetic field

concepts.label: Superconductivity
concepts.definition: Zero electrical resistance below critical temperature

concepts.label: Dielectric
concepts.definition: Insulating material that can be polarized by electric field

concepts.label: Lorentz Force
concepts.definition: Force on charged particle from electric and magnetic fields: F = q(E + v×B)

concepts.label: Speed of Light
concepts.definition: 299,792,458 m/s in vacuum, maximum speed in universe

concepts.label: Refraction
concepts.definition: Bending of light when passing between media of different density

concepts.label: Diffraction
concepts.definition: Bending of waves around obstacles or through slits

concepts.label: Interference
concepts.definition: Superposition of waves resulting in constructive or destructive patterns

concepts.label: Polarization
concepts.definition: Orientation of oscillations of a transverse wave

concepts.label: Special Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory: laws of physics same in all inertial frames, c is constant

concepts.label: General Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory: gravity is curvature of spacetime caused by mass-energy

concepts.label: Mass-Energy Equivalence
concepts.definition: E = mc², mass and energy are interchangeable

concepts.label: Time Dilation
concepts.definition: Time passes slower for objects moving at high speed or in strong gravity

concepts.label: Length Contraction
concepts.definition: Objects contract along direction of motion at relativistic speeds

concepts.label: Spacetime
concepts.definition: Four-dimensional continuum combining space and time

concepts.label: Black Hole
concepts.definition: Region of spacetime with gravity so strong nothing can escape

concepts.label: Event Horizon
concepts.definition: Boundary of black hole beyond which nothing can return

concepts.label: Hawking Radiation
concepts.definition: Theoretical radiation emitted by black holes due to quantum effects

concepts.label: Dark Matter
concepts.definition: Invisible matter accounting for ~27% of universe's mass-energy

concepts.label: Dark Energy
concepts.definition: Unknown energy accelerating universe's expansion, ~68% of universe

concepts.label: Big Bang
concepts.definition: Theory that universe began from an extremely hot dense state ~13.8 billion years ago

concepts.label: Cosmic Microwave Background
concepts.definition: Radiation remnant from early universe, ~2.7K

concepts.label: Nuclear Fusion
concepts.definition: Combining light nuclei to form heavier ones, releasing energy

concepts.label: Nuclear Fission
concepts.definition: Splitting heavy nuclei into lighter ones, releasing energy

concepts.label: Radioactive Decay
concepts.definition: Spontaneous emission of particles or energy from unstable nuclei

concepts.label: Half-Life
concepts.definition: Time for half of radioactive atoms in a sample to decay

concepts.label: Alpha Decay
concepts.definition: Emission of helium nucleus from radioactive atom

concepts.label: Beta Decay
concepts.definition: Emission of electron or positron from neutron/proton conversion

concepts.label: Gamma Decay
concepts.definition: Emission of gamma ray photon from excited nucleus

concepts.label: Doppler Effect
concepts.definition: Change in frequency of wave relative to moving observer

concepts.label: Redshift
concepts.definition: Increase in wavelength of light from objects moving away

concepts.label: Wavelength
concepts.definition: Distance between successive crests of a wave

concepts.label: Frequency
concepts.definition: Number of wave cycles per second, measured in Hertz

concepts.label: Amplitude
concepts.definition: Maximum displacement of a wave from equilibrium

concepts.label: Resonance
concepts.definition: Amplification when driving frequency matches natural frequency

concepts.label: Harmonics
concepts.definition: Integer multiples of a fundamental frequency

concepts.label: Standing Wave
concepts.definition: Wave pattern that appears stationary, formed by interference

concepts.label: Sound Wave
concepts.definition: Longitudinal pressure wave that travels through a medium

concepts.label: Acoustic Resonance
concepts.definition: Amplification of sound at natural frequency of an object

concepts.label: Albert Einstein
concepts.definition: Physicist who developed relativity and contributed to quantum mechanics

concepts.label: James Clerk Maxwell
concepts.definition: Physicist who unified electricity and magnetism

concepts.label: DNA
concepts.definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid, double-helix molecule carrying genetic information

concepts.label: RNA
concepts.definition: Ribonucleic acid, single-stranded molecule involved in protein synthesis

concepts.label: mRNA
concepts.definition: Messenger RNA carrying genetic code from DNA to ribosome

concepts.label: tRNA
concepts.definition: Transfer RNA bringing amino acids to ribosome during translation

concepts.label: rRNA
concepts.definition: Ribosomal RNA forming structural component of ribosomes

concepts.label: DNA Replication
concepts.definition: Process of copying DNA before cell division using DNA polymerase

concepts.label: DNA Polymerase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that synthesizes new DNA strand from template

concepts.label: Helicase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that unwinds DNA double helix during replication

concepts.label: Primase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that creates RNA primers for DNA replication

concepts.label: Ligase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that joins DNA fragments (Okazaki fragments) together

concepts.label: Okazaki Fragment
concepts.definition: Short DNA fragments on lagging strand during replication

concepts.label: Leading Strand
concepts.definition: Continuously synthesized DNA strand during replication

concepts.label: Lagging Strand
concepts.definition: Discontinuously synthesized DNA strand via Okazaki fragments

concepts.label: Transcription
concepts.definition: Process of copying DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase

concepts.label: RNA Polymerase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that synthesizes RNA from DNA template

concepts.label: Promoter
concepts.definition: DNA sequence where RNA polymerase binds to start transcription

concepts.label: Translation
concepts.definition: Process of building protein from mRNA at ribosome

concepts.label: Ribosome
concepts.definition: Cellular organelle that synthesizes proteins from mRNA

concepts.label: Codon
concepts.definition: Three-nucleotide sequence in mRNA coding for one amino acid

concepts.label: Anticodon
concepts.definition: Three-nucleotide sequence on tRNA complementary to mRNA codon

concepts.label: Start Codon
concepts.definition: AUG codon initiating translation, codes for methionine

concepts.label: Stop Codon
concepts.definition: Codons (UAA, UAG, UGA) signaling end of translation

concepts.label: Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Building block of proteins, 20 standard types

concepts.label: Protein
concepts.definition: Large molecule made of amino acid chains, performs most cell functions

concepts.label: Protein Folding
concepts.definition: Process by which protein assumes its functional 3D structure

concepts.label: Protein Synthesis
concepts.definition: Overall process of creating proteins from genetic information

concepts.label: Gene
concepts.definition: Segment of DNA encoding a functional product (usually protein)

concepts.label: Genome
concepts.definition: Complete set of genetic material in an organism

concepts.label: Chromosome
concepts.definition: Structure of coiled DNA and proteins carrying genetic information

concepts.label: Allele
concepts.definition: Variant form of a gene at a specific locus

concepts.label: Mutation
concepts.definition: Change in DNA sequence that may affect gene function

concepts.label: Point Mutation
concepts.definition: Single nucleotide change in DNA sequence

concepts.label: Frameshift Mutation
concepts.definition: Insertion or deletion shifting the reading frame of codons

concepts.label: Gene Expression
concepts.definition: Process by which gene information becomes functional product

concepts.label: Epigenetics
concepts.definition: Heritable changes in gene expression without DNA sequence changes

concepts.label: DNA Methylation
concepts.definition: Addition of methyl group to DNA, often silencing gene expression

concepts.label: CRISPR
concepts.definition: Gene editing technology using bacterial immune system mechanism

concepts.label: Central Dogma
concepts.definition: DNA → RNA → Protein flow of genetic information

concepts.label: Nucleotide
concepts.definition: Building block of DNA/RNA: base + sugar + phosphate

concepts.label: Adenine
concepts.definition: Purine base pairing with thymine (DNA) or uracil (RNA)

concepts.label: Thymine
concepts.definition: Pyrimidine base in DNA pairing with adenine

concepts.label: Guanine
concepts.definition: Purine base pairing with cytosine

concepts.label: Cytosine
concepts.definition: Pyrimidine base pairing with guanine

concepts.label: Uracil
concepts.definition: Pyrimidine base in RNA replacing thymine

concepts.label: Molecular Biology
concepts.definition: Study of biology at the molecular level

concepts.label: Cell
concepts.definition: Basic unit of life, smallest entity that can replicate independently

concepts.label: Eukaryotic Cell
concepts.definition: Cell with membrane-bound nucleus and organelles

concepts.label: Prokaryotic Cell
concepts.definition: Cell without membrane-bound nucleus (bacteria, archaea)

concepts.label: Cell Membrane
concepts.definition: Phospholipid bilayer surrounding cells, controlling substance passage

concepts.label: Nucleus
concepts.definition: Membrane-bound organelle containing DNA in eukaryotes

concepts.label: Mitochondria
concepts.definition: Organelle producing ATP via cellular respiration, has own DNA

concepts.label: Endoplasmic Reticulum
concepts.definition: Organelle network for protein and lipid synthesis

concepts.label: Golgi Apparatus
concepts.definition: Organelle modifying, packaging, and shipping proteins

concepts.label: Lysosome
concepts.definition: Organelle containing digestive enzymes for waste breakdown

concepts.label: Chloroplast
concepts.definition: Plant organelle performing photosynthesis, contains chlorophyll

concepts.label: Cytoplasm
concepts.definition: Gel-like substance inside cell membrane containing organelles

concepts.label: Cytoskeleton
concepts.definition: Network of protein filaments providing cell structure and movement

concepts.label: Cell Division
concepts.definition: Process of cell reproducing into two daughter cells

concepts.label: Mitosis
concepts.definition: Cell division producing two identical diploid daughter cells

concepts.label: Meiosis
concepts.definition: Cell division producing four haploid gametes with genetic variation

concepts.label: Apoptosis
concepts.definition: Programmed cell death, essential for development and homeostasis

concepts.label: Stem Cell
concepts.definition: Undifferentiated cell capable of becoming specialized cell types

concepts.label: Cell Signaling
concepts.definition: Process by which cells communicate using chemical signals

concepts.label: Signal Transduction
concepts.definition: Process of converting extracellular signal to cellular response

concepts.label: Receptor
concepts.definition: Protein on or in cell that binds specific signal molecules

concepts.label: Ligand
concepts.definition: Molecule that binds to a receptor to trigger a response

concepts.label: Second Messenger
concepts.definition: Intracellular signaling molecule (e.g. cAMP, Ca²⁺)

concepts.label: ATP
concepts.definition: Adenosine triphosphate, primary energy currency of cells

concepts.label: Cellular Respiration
concepts.definition: Process converting glucose and O₂ to ATP, CO₂, and H₂O

concepts.label: Glycolysis
concepts.definition: Breakdown of glucose to pyruvate yielding 2 ATP, occurs in cytoplasm

concepts.label: Krebs Cycle
concepts.definition: Citric acid cycle generating electron carriers in mitochondria

concepts.label: Electron Transport Chain
concepts.definition: Series of proteins transferring electrons to generate ATP

concepts.label: Oxidative Phosphorylation
concepts.definition: ATP production using energy from electron transport chain

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: Process converting CO₂ and water to glucose using light energy

concepts.label: Calvin Cycle
concepts.definition: Light-independent reactions fixing CO₂ into glucose in chloroplast

concepts.label: Fermentation
concepts.definition: Anaerobic metabolic process producing energy without oxygen

concepts.label: Osmosis
concepts.definition: Diffusion of water across semipermeable membrane

concepts.label: Diffusion
concepts.definition: Movement of particles from high to low concentration

concepts.label: Active Transport
concepts.definition: Movement of substances against concentration gradient using energy

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: Biological catalyst that speeds up chemical reactions

concepts.label: Enzyme Substrate Complex
concepts.definition: Temporary binding of enzyme and substrate at active site

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: Substance that speeds up reaction without being consumed

concepts.label: Cell Biology
concepts.definition: Study of cell structure and function

concepts.label: Immune System
concepts.definition: Body's defense system against pathogens and disease

concepts.label: Innate Immunity
concepts.definition: Non-specific first-line defense present from birth

concepts.label: Adaptive Immunity
concepts.definition: Specific immune response that develops after exposure

concepts.label: White Blood Cell
concepts.definition: Immune cell (leukocyte) defending body against infection

concepts.label: T Cell
concepts.definition: Lymphocyte maturing in thymus, key to adaptive immunity

concepts.label: Helper T Cell
concepts.definition: T cell coordinating immune response by releasing cytokines

concepts.label: Killer T Cell
concepts.definition: Cytotoxic T cell destroying infected or cancerous cells

concepts.label: B Cell
concepts.definition: Lymphocyte producing antibodies for humoral immunity

concepts.label: Antibody
concepts.definition: Y-shaped protein binding specific antigens to neutralize threats

concepts.label: Antigen
concepts.definition: Molecule that triggers an immune response

concepts.label: Macrophage
concepts.definition: Large immune cell engulfing and digesting pathogens (phagocytosis)

concepts.label: Neutrophil
concepts.definition: Most common white blood cell, first responder to infection

concepts.label: Natural Killer Cell
concepts.definition: Innate immune cell killing virus-infected and tumor cells

concepts.label: Cytokine
concepts.definition: Signaling protein coordinating immune response

concepts.label: Inflammation
concepts.definition: Immune response causing redness, swelling, heat, pain at injury site

concepts.label: Fever
concepts.definition: Elevated body temperature as immune defense against pathogens

concepts.label: Vaccine
concepts.definition: Preparation that stimulates adaptive immunity against specific pathogen

concepts.label: Immunization
concepts.definition: Process of becoming immune through vaccination or exposure

concepts.label: Autoimmune Disease
concepts.definition: Condition where immune system attacks body's own tissues

concepts.label: Allergy
concepts.definition: Immune overreaction to harmless substances (allergens)

concepts.label: Histamine
concepts.definition: Chemical released during allergic reactions causing inflammation

concepts.label: Lymph Node
concepts.definition: Small organ filtering lymph fluid and housing immune cells

concepts.label: Thymus
concepts.definition: Organ where T cells mature, located behind sternum

concepts.label: Bone Marrow
concepts.definition: Tissue inside bones producing blood cells including immune cells

concepts.label: Phagocytosis
concepts.definition: Process of cell engulfing and digesting particles or pathogens

concepts.label: Complement System
concepts.definition: Group of proteins enhancing ability of antibodies to clear pathogens

concepts.label: Interferon
concepts.definition: Protein released by cells in response to viral infection

concepts.label: MHC
concepts.definition: Major histocompatibility complex, presents antigens to T cells

concepts.label: Memory Cell
concepts.definition: Long-lived B or T cell providing rapid response on re-exposure

concepts.label: Pathogen
concepts.definition: Microorganism causing disease (bacteria, virus, fungus, parasite)

concepts.label: Virus
concepts.definition: Infectious agent replicating only inside living cells

concepts.label: Bacteria
concepts.definition: Single-celled prokaryotic microorganism

concepts.label: Antibiotic
concepts.definition: Substance killing or inhibiting bacteria growth

concepts.label: Antibiotic Resistance
concepts.definition: Ability of bacteria to survive antibiotic treatment through evolution

concepts.label: Neuroscience
concepts.definition: Study of the nervous system and brain

concepts.label: Neuron
concepts.definition: Nerve cell transmitting electrical and chemical signals

concepts.label: Synapse
concepts.definition: Junction between two neurons where signals are transmitted

concepts.label: Axon
concepts.definition: Long projection of neuron conducting impulses away from cell body

concepts.label: Dendrite
concepts.definition: Branching projection of neuron receiving signals from other neurons

concepts.label: Myelin Sheath
concepts.definition: Insulating layer around axon speeding up signal transmission

concepts.label: Action Potential
concepts.definition: Rapid electrical signal traveling along neuron's axon

concepts.label: Neurotransmitter
concepts.definition: Chemical messenger released at synapse to transmit signals

concepts.label: Dopamine
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter involved in reward, motivation, and movement

concepts.label: Serotonin
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter regulating mood, sleep, and appetite

concepts.label: GABA
concepts.definition: Primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain

concepts.label: Glutamate
concepts.definition: Primary excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain

concepts.label: Norepinephrine
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter involved in alertness and fight-or-flight response

concepts.label: Acetylcholine
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter involved in muscle contraction and memory

concepts.label: Endorphin
concepts.definition: Neuropeptide reducing pain and producing euphoria

concepts.label: Oxytocin
concepts.definition: Hormone/neurotransmitter involved in bonding and trust

concepts.label: Cortisol
concepts.definition: Stress hormone produced by adrenal glands

concepts.label: Melatonin
concepts.definition: Hormone regulating sleep-wake cycle, produced by pineal gland

concepts.label: Neuroplasticity
concepts.definition: Brain's ability to reorganize and form new neural connections

concepts.label: Long-Term Potentiation
concepts.definition: Strengthening of synapses based on repeated stimulation

concepts.label: Synaptic Plasticity
concepts.definition: Ability of synapses to strengthen or weaken over time

concepts.label: Nervous System
concepts.definition: Body system controlling actions and transmitting signals

concepts.label: Central Nervous System
concepts.definition: Brain and spinal cord

concepts.label: Peripheral Nervous System
concepts.definition: Nerves outside brain and spinal cord

concepts.label: Autonomic Nervous System
concepts.definition: Controls involuntary functions (heart rate, digestion)

concepts.label: Sympathetic Nervous System
concepts.definition: Fight-or-flight response division of autonomic nervous system

concepts.label: Density
concepts.definition: Mass per unit volume of a substance (ρ = m/V)

concepts.label: Buoyancy
concepts.definition: Upward force on object in fluid equal to weight of displaced fluid

concepts.label: Archimedes' Principle
concepts.definition: Object in fluid experiences upward force equal to weight of displaced fluid

concepts.label: Viscosity
concepts.definition: Measure of fluid's resistance to flow

concepts.label: Surface Tension
concepts.definition: Tendency of liquid surface to minimize area due to cohesive forces

concepts.label: Pressure
concepts.definition: Force per unit area (P = F/A)

concepts.label: Pascal's Principle
concepts.definition: Pressure applied to confined fluid transmits equally in all directions

concepts.label: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.definition: Faster fluid flow means lower pressure

concepts.label: Torque
concepts.definition: Rotational force: τ = r × F

concepts.label: Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Rotational equivalent of linear momentum: L = Iω

concepts.label: Moment of Inertia
concepts.definition: Resistance to rotational acceleration depending on mass distribution

concepts.label: Center of Mass
concepts.definition: Average position of mass in a system

concepts.label: Momentum
concepts.definition: Product of mass and velocity: p = mv

concepts.label: Impulse
concepts.definition: Change in momentum equal to force times time: J = FΔt

concepts.label: Conservation of Momentum
concepts.definition: Total momentum of isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Conservation of Energy
concepts.definition: Total energy of isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Kinetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of motion: KE = ½mv²

concepts.label: Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to position or configuration

concepts.label: Gravitational Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy from position in gravitational field: PE = mgh

concepts.label: Elastic Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored in stretched or compressed elastic material

concepts.label: Work
concepts.definition: Energy transfer by force over distance: W = Fd cos θ

concepts.label: Power
concepts.definition: Rate of doing work or transferring energy: P = W/t

concepts.label: Efficiency
concepts.definition: Ratio of useful output to total input energy

concepts.label: Newton's First Law
concepts.definition: Object at rest stays at rest unless acted on by external force (inertia)

concepts.label: Newton's Second Law
concepts.definition: Force equals mass times acceleration: F = ma

concepts.label: Newton's Third Law
concepts.definition: Every action has equal and opposite reaction

concepts.label: Inertia
concepts.definition: Tendency of object to resist change in motion

concepts.label: Acceleration
concepts.definition: Rate of change of velocity over time

concepts.label: Velocity
concepts.definition: Speed in a given direction

concepts.label: Displacement
concepts.definition: Change in position of an object

concepts.label: Force
concepts.definition: Push or pull that can change object's motion

concepts.label: Weight
concepts.definition: Gravitational force on an object: W = mg

concepts.label: Mass
concepts.definition: Quantity of matter in an object

concepts.label: Volume
concepts.definition: Amount of three-dimensional space occupied

concepts.label: Area
concepts.definition: Measure of two-dimensional surface

concepts.label: Ratio
concepts.definition: Quantitative relation between two amounts: a/b

concepts.label: Proportion
concepts.definition: Statement that two ratios are equal: a/b = c/d

concepts.label: Percentage
concepts.definition: Ratio expressed as fraction of 100

concepts.label: Logarithm
concepts.definition: Inverse operation of exponentiation: log_b(x) = y means b^y = x

concepts.label: Exponential Growth
concepts.definition: Growth rate proportional to current value, doubling pattern

concepts.label: Exponential Decay
concepts.definition: Decrease at rate proportional to current value

concepts.label: Linear Relationship
concepts.definition: Relationship where change in one variable produces proportional change in another

concepts.label: Inverse Relationship
concepts.definition: As one variable increases, the other decreases proportionally

concepts.label: Correlation
concepts.definition: Statistical relationship between two variables

concepts.label: Causation
concepts.definition: One event directly causes another

concepts.label: Probability
concepts.definition: Measure of likelihood of an event occurring (0-1)

concepts.label: Statistics
concepts.definition: Collection, analysis, and interpretation of numerical data

concepts.label: Mean
concepts.definition: Average value: sum of values divided by count

concepts.label: Median
concepts.definition: Middle value in ordered dataset

concepts.label: Standard Deviation
concepts.definition: Measure of data spread around the mean

concepts.label: Normal Distribution
concepts.definition: Bell-shaped probability distribution

concepts.label: Variance
concepts.definition: Square of standard deviation, measure of data spread

concepts.label: Sample Size
concepts.definition: Number of observations in a statistical sample

concepts.label: Hypothesis
concepts.definition: Testable prediction about relationship between variables

concepts.label: Scientific Method
concepts.definition: Systematic approach: observe, hypothesize, test, conclude

concepts.label: Control Variable
concepts.definition: Variable kept constant in an experiment

concepts.label: Independent Variable
concepts.definition: Variable deliberately changed in an experiment

concepts.label: Dependent Variable
concepts.definition: Variable measured as outcome in an experiment

concepts.label: Dimensional Analysis
concepts.definition: Technique using units to check equations and convert measurements

concepts.label: Order of Magnitude
concepts.definition: Approximate measure of size expressed as power of 10

concepts.label: Significant Figures
concepts.definition: Digits carrying meaningful information about measurement precision

concepts.label: Vector
concepts.definition: Quantity with both magnitude and direction

concepts.label: Scalar
concepts.definition: Quantity with magnitude only, no direction

concepts.label: Coordinate System
concepts.definition: System for specifying position using numbers

concepts.label: Trigonometry
concepts.definition: Mathematics of triangles and angular relationships

concepts.label: Sine
concepts.definition: Trigonometric function: opposite/hypotenuse

concepts.label: Cosine
concepts.definition: Trigonometric function: adjacent/hypotenuse

concepts.label: Calculus
concepts.definition: Mathematics of rates of change and accumulation

concepts.label: Derivative
concepts.definition: Rate of change of function, slope of tangent line

concepts.label: Integral
concepts.definition: Accumulation of quantity, area under curve

concepts.label: Limit
concepts.definition: Value a function approaches as input approaches some value

concepts.label: Infinity
concepts.definition: Concept of unbounded quantity, larger than any number

concepts.label: Pi
concepts.definition: Ratio of circle's circumference to diameter ≈ 3.14159

concepts.label: Golden Ratio
concepts.definition: Irrational number φ ≈ 1.618, appears in nature and art

concepts.label: Fibonacci Sequence
concepts.definition: Sequence where each number is sum of two preceding: 1,1,2,3,5,8...

concepts.label: Fractal
concepts.definition: Self-similar pattern repeating at different scales

concepts.label: Chaos Theory
concepts.definition: Study of deterministic systems highly sensitive to initial conditions

concepts.label: Butterfly Effect
concepts.definition: Small change causing large differences in later state (chaos theory)

concepts.label: Feedback Loop
concepts.definition: System where output feeds back as input

concepts.label: Positive Feedback
concepts.definition: Feedback amplifying original change

concepts.label: Negative Feedback
concepts.definition: Feedback counteracting original change, stabilizing system

concepts.label: Homeostasis
concepts.definition: Biological process maintaining stable internal conditions

concepts.label: Equilibrium
concepts.definition: State of balance between opposing forces or processes

concepts.label: Steady State
concepts.definition: Condition where system variables remain constant over time

concepts.label: Elasticity
concepts.definition: Ability of material to return to original shape after deformation

concepts.label: Plasticity
concepts.definition: Permanent deformation of material under stress

concepts.label: Tensile Strength
concepts.definition: Maximum stress a material can withstand before breaking

concepts.label: Stress
concepts.definition: Force per unit area applied to a material

concepts.label: Strain
concepts.definition: Deformation of material relative to original dimensions

concepts.label: Hooke's Law
concepts.definition: Force proportional to displacement in elastic materials: F = -kx

concepts.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
concepts.definition: Periodic motion with restoring force proportional to displacement

concepts.label: Pendulum
concepts.definition: Weight on string exhibiting periodic motion under gravity

concepts.label: Oscillation
concepts.definition: Repetitive variation about equilibrium point

concepts.label: Period
concepts.definition: Time for one complete cycle of periodic motion

concepts.label: Hertz
concepts.definition: Unit of frequency: one cycle per second

concepts.label: Decibel
concepts.definition: Logarithmic unit measuring sound intensity

concepts.label: Spectrum
concepts.definition: Range of related values or properties

concepts.label: Symmetry
concepts.definition: Property of being unchanged under transformation

concepts.label: Conservation Law
concepts.definition: Physical quantity remaining constant in isolated system

concepts.label: Entropy (Information)
concepts.definition: Measure of uncertainty or information content

concepts.label: Signal-to-Noise Ratio
concepts.definition: Ratio of desired signal to background noise

concepts.label: Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Material with conductivity between conductor and insulator

concepts.label: Transistor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor device for switching/amplifying electronic signals

concepts.label: Integrated Circuit
concepts.definition: Set of electronic circuits on small chip of semiconductor

concepts.label: Moore's Law
concepts.definition: Observation that transistor count doubles roughly every 2 years

concepts.label: Algorithm
concepts.definition: Step-by-step procedure for solving a problem

concepts.label: Complexity
concepts.definition: Amount of resources needed to solve a problem

concepts.label: Network Effect
concepts.definition: Product/service gains value as more people use it

concepts.label: Emergence
concepts.definition: Complex patterns arising from simple rules/interactions

concepts.label: Systems Thinking
concepts.definition: Holistic approach understanding how components interrelate

concepts.label: Reductionism
concepts.definition: Approach explaining complex systems by their simplest parts

concepts.label: Hemoglobin
concepts.definition: Protein in red blood cells binding oxygen for transport

concepts.label: Bone
concepts.definition: Hard connective tissue forming skeleton

concepts.label: Muscle
concepts.definition: Tissue that contracts to produce movement

concepts.label: Tendon
concepts.definition: Connective tissue attaching muscle to bone

concepts.label: Cartilage
concepts.definition: Flexible connective tissue in joints and structures

concepts.label: Hormone
concepts.definition: Chemical messenger transported in blood regulating body functions

concepts.label: Endocrine System
concepts.definition: System of glands producing hormones

concepts.label: Pituitary Gland
concepts.definition: Master gland controlling other endocrine glands

concepts.label: Adrenal Gland
concepts.definition: Gland producing adrenaline and cortisol

concepts.label: Thyroid Gland
concepts.definition: Gland regulating metabolism via thyroid hormones

concepts.label: Pancreas
concepts.definition: Organ producing insulin and digestive enzymes

concepts.label: Liver
concepts.definition: Organ for detoxification, bile production, and metabolism

concepts.label: Kidney
concepts.definition: Organ filtering blood and producing urine

concepts.label: Lung
concepts.definition: Organ for gas exchange: oxygen in, CO2 out

concepts.label: Heart
concepts.definition: Muscular organ pumping blood through circulatory system

concepts.label: Blood
concepts.definition: Fluid transporting oxygen, nutrients, and waste in body

concepts.label: Red Blood Cell
concepts.definition: Cell carrying oxygen via hemoglobin

concepts.label: Platelet
concepts.definition: Cell fragment involved in blood clotting

concepts.label: Plasma
concepts.definition: Liquid component of blood carrying cells and proteins

concepts.label: Digestive System
concepts.definition: Organ system breaking down food for absorption

concepts.label: Stomach
concepts.definition: Organ breaking down food with acid and enzymes

concepts.label: Intestine
concepts.definition: Organ for nutrient absorption (small) and water absorption (large)

concepts.label: Microbiome
concepts.definition: Community of microorganisms living in/on the body

concepts.label: Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Community of organisms interacting with their environment

concepts.label: Food Web
concepts.definition: Network of interconnected food chains in ecosystem

concepts.label: Producer
concepts.definition: Organism making own food via photosynthesis (autotroph)

concepts.label: Consumer
concepts.definition: Organism feeding on other organisms (heterotroph)

concepts.label: Decomposer
concepts.definition: Organism breaking down dead matter and recycling nutrients

concepts.label: Nitrogen Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle of nitrogen through atmosphere and organisms

concepts.label: Carbon Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle of carbon through atmosphere, biosphere, ocean

concepts.label: Water Cycle
concepts.definition: Continuous movement of water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation

concepts.label: Symbiosis
concepts.definition: Close long-term interaction between different species

concepts.label: Mutualism
concepts.definition: Symbiotic relationship benefiting both species

concepts.label: Parasitism
concepts.definition: Symbiotic relationship where one benefits at other's expense

concepts.label: Predation
concepts.definition: One organism killing and eating another

concepts.label: Competition
concepts.definition: Organisms competing for same limited resources

concepts.label: Carrying Capacity
concepts.definition: Maximum population size an environment can sustain indefinitely

concepts.label: Population Dynamics
concepts.definition: Study of how populations change in size and composition

concepts.label: Natural Resource
concepts.definition: Material from nature used by humans (water, minerals, forests)

concepts.label: Sustainability
concepts.definition: Meeting present needs without compromising future generations

concepts.label: Renewable Resource
concepts.definition: Resource that can be replenished naturally (solar, wind, water)

concepts.label: Non-Renewable Resource
concepts.definition: Resource that cannot be replenished on human timescale (fossil fuels)

concepts.label: Photovoltaic Cell
concepts.definition: Device converting light directly into electricity

concepts.label: Battery
concepts.definition: Device storing chemical energy and converting to electrical energy

concepts.label: Capacitor
concepts.definition: Device storing electrical energy in electric field

concepts.label: Transformer
concepts.definition: Device changing voltage of alternating current

concepts.label: Electric Generator
concepts.definition: Device converting mechanical energy to electrical energy

concepts.label: Electric Motor
concepts.definition: Device converting electrical energy to mechanical energy

concepts.label: Lever
concepts.definition: Simple machine: rigid bar rotating around fulcrum

concepts.label: Pulley
concepts.definition: Simple machine: wheel with rope reducing force needed

concepts.label: Inclined Plane
concepts.definition: Simple machine: flat surface tilted at angle

concepts.label: Mechanical Advantage
concepts.definition: Factor by which machine multiplies input force

concepts.label: Gear
concepts.definition: Rotating machine part with teeth for transmitting motion

concepts.label: Thermal Conductivity
concepts.definition: Material's ability to conduct heat

concepts.label: Electrical Conductivity
concepts.definition: Material's ability to conduct electric current

concepts.label: Insulator
concepts.definition: Material that resists flow of heat or electricity

concepts.label: Conductor
concepts.definition: Material allowing easy flow of heat or electricity

concepts.label: Optics
concepts.definition: Branch of physics studying light behavior

concepts.label: Lens
concepts.definition: Transparent optical element refracting light

concepts.label: Focal Length
concepts.definition: Distance from lens to point where parallel rays converge

concepts.label: Magnification
concepts.definition: Ratio of image size to object size

concepts.label: Microscope
concepts.definition: Instrument magnifying small objects

concepts.label: Telescope
concepts.definition: Instrument viewing distant objects
